author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.

classification.cautionReason: This work discusses heresy in order to refute it; do not classify it as heretical merely from its title.

lineage: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.
He Suggests a Dilemma to Cœlestius.
title: He Suggests a Dilemma to Cœlestius.

verses.book: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.
verses.text: What was that which the same pope replied to the bishops of Numidia concerning this very cause, because he had received letters from both Councils, as well from the Council of Carthage as from the Council of Mileve—does he not speak most plainly concerning infants?

verses.book: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.
verses.text: For these are his words: “For what your Fraternity asserts that they preach, that infants can be endowed with the rewards of eternal life even without the grace of baptism, is excessively silly; for unless they shall eat the flesh of the Son of man, and drink His blood, they shall not have life in themselves.

verses.book: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.
verses.text: And they who maintain this as being theirs without regeneration, appear to me to wish to destroy baptism itself, since they proclaim that these have that which we believe is not to be conferred on them without baptism.”

verses.book: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.
verses.text: What does the ungrateful man say to this, when the Apostolic See had already spared him on his profession, as if he were corrected by its most benignant lenity?

verses.book: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.
verses.text: What does he say to this?

verses.book: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.
verses.text: Will infants after the end of their life, even if while they live they are not baptized in Christ, be in eternal life, or will they not?

verses.book: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.
verses.text: If he should say, “They will,” how then did he answer that he had condemned what had been uttered under his name “according to the judgment of Innocent, of blessed memory”?

verses.book: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.
verses.text: Lo, Pope Innocent, of blessed memory, says that infants have not life without Christ’s baptism, and without partaking of Christ’s body and blood.

verses.book: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.
verses.text: If he should say, “They will not,” how then, if they do not receive eternal life, are they certainly by consequence condemned in eternal death if they derive no original sin?
